etymology.description: Phonetic: 𡍬 (phonetic component, evolved from 士(士) (shì, 'scholar') and 四(四) (sì, 'four')) + Semantic: 貝(貝) (bèi, 'cowry') -> to sell; trade
etymology.interpretation: The character 𧶠 combines the phonetic element 𡍬, which later simplified into components 士(士) (shì, 'scholar') and 四(四) (sì, 'four'), with 貝(貝) (bèi, 'cowry'), indicating a connection to trade and the use of cowry shells as money. It is related in meaning to the character 賣(賣) (mài, 'sell').
